title: Sybase Identity Fields
text: Sybase Identity Fields Sybase only allows one Identity field for each table. Insert and Update If the identity insert connector option is not set, the Sybase DBMS controls the value of the Identity field. The connector does not update this field, it only inserts a value into the Identity field if the identity insert connector option is set. Therefore, when the identity insert connector option is not set, entities which display Identity fields must re-fetch the Identity value after each write so that Uniface gets the correct value for the Identity field. To display the correct identity value after an insert or update, the following should be added to the Write trigger, for entities which contain Identity fields: ;trigger Write store sql "select syb_identity from table1 where pk = %% pk_in_cmp", "syb" ident_field = $result Copy where pk is the primary key of the table and pk_in_cmp is the primary key field of the component. Uniface Warnings If the identity insert connector option is not set, Uniface issues the following warning message when storing a value in an Identity field: 2012 - Occurrence mismatch. Copy This is because the value for the Identity field displayed on the component before the store, does not match the value in the database after the store. This message can be ignored. Identity insert When the identity insert connector option is set, the SYB connector sets the Sybase DBMS identity_insert option for each table which contains an Identity field. When the Sybase DBMS identity_insert option is set, the Sybase DBMS allows users to insert duplicate Identity values unless the Identity field is in a unique index (a Uniface primary or candidate key). The identity insert connector option is useful in the following situations: If the user wants to plant a base (seed) value which Sybase should use 0as the lowest Identity value for a table. If the user wants to perform a Data conversion into a table which has an Identity field and requires a Uniface application to control the values of the Identity field. Otherwise, the Sybase DBMS provides a sequential number based on the order of insertion. Primary and Candidate Keys If an Identity field is in a primary or candidate key that is displayed on a form, a valid Identity value must be entered even if the identity insert connector option is not set. The reason is that Uniface requires that a value be entered for all mandatory fields. The one exception is when the identity insert connector option is not set, and retrieve/o is used for a table with an Identity field in the primary or candidate key. In this situation, entering 0 is recommended. The reason for this is that the SYB connector does not use the Identity value for an actual insertion value, though it must check if the primary key already exists in the DBMS. Limitations of Identity Fields Identity fields cannot be used in the primary key of a table which requires an overflow table. Identity fields cannot be used in a relationship. Therefore, they can never be part of a foreign key, and they cannot be defined in a primary or candidate key which participates in a relationship.
